name: Woody Shaw
abstract: Woody Herman Shaw Jr. (December 24, 1944 – May 10, 1989) was an American jazz trumpeter, flugelhornist, cornetist, composer, arranger, band leader, and educator.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Art Blakey and His Jazz Messengers
Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music
Kool Jazz Festival: The Blakey Legacy

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: I transcribed him. And so when I got to NEC my teacher... told me about all these other trumpet players I needed to listen to. So what I ended up doing is, you know, going out and finding records and recordings and going to the library and listening to LPs of all the players that influenced the play
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: So I put one together... and then we got Woody Shaw. So it was the Louis Hayes-Junior Cook Quintet featuring Woody Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first bands I put together that I was real excited about was the band that finally made my first recording, Tones For Joan’s Bones, with Steve Swallow, Joe Farrell, Joe Chambers, and Woody Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: You want to join my band? ... I loved those guys, so if nothing else I’ll have some great cats to work with ... Woody became a friend as the years progressed, only maybe two years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Aebersold Jamey
about_quotes.evidence: On the Woody Shaw, Volume 9 there, that you’re looking at, we recorded it here in the basement. He was over in one little room here, with headphones on, and he was soloing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Aebersold Jamey — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I had – I think at that time I was using Woody Shaw, Scotty Holt, LaMont Johnson, and Billy Higgins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
